description:
Goal
Make compiler2's semantic convergence spreadsheet-shaped. Each job reads the minimum current facts needed to do its work, waits only for missing facts that actually block that work, and publishes only evidence it can justify. Readiness is represented by the fact graph (`Current`/`Settled`/absence/dirty publishers); type values represent program value sets only. `any` and `none` are never readiness placeholders.

Signal
- The current uncommitted activation/readiness batch is landed through precise tickets or explicitly discarded before the ReturnEvidence lane starts.
- Quicksort, Enum.reduce/range/list, Enum.sort, and Enum.to_list/map converge under hard timeouts with compact meaningful-event budgets.
- Semantic jobs can publish activation demand/edge evidence before return types are known, while typed consumers wait for the exact return/callsite evidence they need.
- Dynamic activation cycles are derived from activation dataflow and converge by widening the semantic evidence domain, not by static `Recursive(function)` or source-shape guesses.
- Return evidence distinguishes fact absence, known concrete return, explicit dynamic boundary, and no-return control flow. Local value evidence uses the same model and does not use `Option<Ty>` as readiness.
- Artifact/backend/native work consumes settled semantic evidence and projects to `Ty` only at named boundaries that truly need a type-shaped value.
- `cargo test --lib compiler2::` passes under a hard timeout, docs match code, and deletion checks prove old concepts are gone rather than renamed.

Strategy
1. Finish `fz-rh2.18.9`: activation partitioning and dynamic convergence. This includes the active `.9.12` missing-return split, the new `.9.13` quicksort artifact-seam blocker, the Enum.to_list/map telemetry budget, and the activation closeout.
2. Execute `fz-rh2.18.6`: replace `ReturnType(Ty)` with explicit `ReturnEvidence`, then replace local semantic `Option<Ty>` pending state with explicit value evidence before tightening call resolution and artifact projection.
3. Execute `fz-rh2.18.7`: audit every remaining `any`/`none` producer and repair the earliest wrong fact/evidence boundary instead of blessing local fallbacks.
4. Execute `fz-rh2.18.8`: close with docs, telemetry, hard timeout commands, and no-go grep checks.

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[fz-rh2.18.9 activation partition + dynamic convergence] --> B[fz-rh2.18.6 ReturnEvidence + local semantic evidence]
  B --> C[fz-rh2.18.7 any/none poison burn-down]
  C --> D[fz-rh2.18.8 docs telemetry deletion contract]
```

Prior Art / Model Fit
Bourdoncle's dynamic partitioning frames the right shape: compute the minimal function graph demanded by the queried inputs, dynamically choose useful partitions, and widen representation chains so they stabilize. In compiler2 terms, activation keys are demanded abstract control points, `ActivationInputs`/return evidence are the abstract values, and fact readiness is the convergence proof.

Outro
No `ActivationKey`-as-body-input, no recursive-only key widening, no static `Recursive(function)` as semantic convergence authority, no list-only `convergence_class`, no raw closure-capture activation keys unless policy proves identity is necessary, no retained-output workaround, no missing-return-as-`none`, no unresolved-as-`any`, no `ReturnType`-as-ready-`Ty`, no local `Option<Ty>` readiness, no stale `DependencySnapshot`/manual closure-freshness model, no debug-payload telemetry budgets, and no unbounded `fz2 --log-telemetry` workflow.
title: Compiler2 fact readiness: current vs settled evidence
